alignments.candidate_evidence: Eight years at Google Devices and Services with two promotions, scaling global commercial operations and leading transformation programs; experience with ownership, requirements, logic design, and rollout
alignments.job_requirement: synthesise disconnected streams of thought into a cohesive understanding of what the most important problem is

alignments.candidate_evidence: Product experience grounded in transformation and UAT; demonstrated ability to work across stakeholder groups and design processes, though not in a traditional consulting brand engagement model
alignments.job_requirement: immerse themselves in our customers' most intricate workflows, partner with customer teams and explore the data

alignments.candidate_evidence: Product operations and product strategy background at scale within Google; experience with requirements logic design and product-level problem solving
alignments.job_requirement: dive into the product landscape to enable us to scale

alignments.candidate_evidence: Positioned as Strategy and Operations / Product Operations leader with transformation and scaling experience; product experience rooted in ownership and business problem solving
alignments.job_requirement: curious and analytical mindset, a sharp intuition for product, and a strong degree of user empathy

alignments.candidate_evidence: Transformation program leadership and commercial operations scaling suggest cross-functional stakeholder management, though prior experience is internal Google-scale rather than external customer deployment
alignments.job_requirement: partner with customer teams to understand the most important problem

gaps.gap: No direct experience as a Deployment Strategist or in a forward-deployed consulting/customer-engagement model; background is internal product operations and transformation at Google rather than external customer deployment
gaps.mitigation: Eight years of product operations and transformation leadership demonstrates core analytical, process design, and stakeholder management capabilities transferable to customer-facing deployment work. Training and mentoring by experienced deployment teams could accelerate ramp

gaps.gap: Limited classic consulting brand and case-team background; no demonstrated experience in rapid-iteration customer engagements or external advisory delivery
gaps.mitigation: Product operations transformation and UAT experience show ability to iterate and deliver across complex stakeholder environments. Palantir's internal deployment training and apprenticeship model could bridge this

gaps.gap: JD does not explicitly specify Australia government domain or deep public-sector experience; candidate background is Google commercial products
gaps.mitigation: Analytical mindset and product operations rigor are domain-portable; government sector domain knowledge can be acquired through onboarding and team mentorship

output.summary: Strong role-family fit for Deployment Strategist (approved stretch family). Candidate's eight years of product operations, transformation program leadership, and cross-functional scaling at Google demonstrate core analytical, stakeholder management, and process design capabilities. Primary gaps are lack of direct customer-facing deployment experience and consulting-style engagement model; these are manageable with onboarding and mentoring. Confidence moderated by unclear JD scope definition and limited explicit data exploration evidence.
uncertainties: Extent of hands-on data exploration and technical data fluency beyond product logic; JD emphasizes 'explore the data' and Python basic may limit depth in early deployment phases
How 'immerse in customer workflows' will translate from internal transformation work at scale to external government customer engagements
Whether Deployment Strategist scope at Palantir is L4-L5 or spans higher IC/leadership levels; title is listed as approved stretch but role ambiguity remains
